name: Hungary
excerpt: Hungary became a Christian kingdom in A.D. 1000 and for many centuries served as a bulwark against Ottoman Turkish expansion in Europe. The kingdom eventually became part of the Austro-Hungarian Empire, which collapsed during World War I.

quick_facts.religions: Catholic 30.1% (Roman Catholic 27.5%, Greek Catholic 1.7%, other Catholic 0.9%), Calvinist 9.8%, Lutheran 1.8%, other Christian (includes Orthodox) 1.6%, other 0.4%, none 16.1%, no answer 40.1% (2022 est.)
quick_facts.ethnic_groups: Hungarian 84.3%, Romani 2.1%, German 1%, other 1.2%, unspecified 13.7% (2022 est.)
quick_facts.climate: temperate; cold, cloudy, humid winters; warm summers
quick_facts.terrain: mostly flat to rolling plains; hills and low mountains on the Slovakian border
quick_facts.natural_resources: bauxite, coal, natural gas, fertile soils, arable land
quick_facts.major_exports: cars, vehicle parts/accessories, electric batteries, packaged medicine, computers (2023)

summaries.geography_summary: landlocked; strategic location on main land routes between Western Europe and Balkan Peninsula, as well as between Ukraine and the Mediterranean basin; the Duna (Danube) and Tisza Rivers divide the country into three large regions
summaries.history_summary: Hungary became a Christian kingdom in A.D. 1000 and for many centuries served as a bulwark against Ottoman Turkish expansion in Europe. The kingdom eventually became part of the Austro-Hungarian Empire, which collapsed during World War I. The country fell under communist rule after World War II. In 1956, Moscow responded to a Hungarian revolt and announcement of its withdrawal from the Warsaw Pact with a massive military intervention. Under the leadership of Janos KADAR in 1968, Hungary began liberalizing its economy, introducing so-called "Goulash Communism." Hungary held its first multiparty elections in 1990 and initiated a free market economy. It joined NATO in 1999 and the EU five years later.
summaries.government_summary: parliamentary republic. Independence: 16 November 1918 (republic proclaimed); notable earlier dates: 25 December 1000 (crowning of King STEPHEN I, traditional founding date); 30 March 1867 (Austro-Hungarian dual monarchy established)
summaries.economy_summary: high-income EU and OECD economy; modest recovery from 2024 recession driven by private consumption and moderated inflation; challenges include high fiscal deficits, frozen access to EU funds, and risks from export reliance; implementing tax exemptions, price controls, and mortgage interest caps ahead of 2026 elections

suggested_reading: CIA World Factbook data (archived via factbook.json) | https://github.com/factbook/factbook.json

prayer_and_mission.prayer_intro: Hungary's Catholic plurality exists alongside a large population that declined to state a religion, suggesting inherited religious identity has weakened considerably. Pray for the clear gospel of salvation by grace through faith alone to reach a nation where religious identity is uncertain for many.
prayer_points: Pray for Hungary's small evangelical and Calvinist Reformed presence to be strengthened.
Pray for nominal Catholics to encounter the actual gospel of salvation by grace through personal faith in Christ.
Pray for Hungarian pastors to be trained in sound, Scripture-centered teaching.
prayer_and_mission.mission_emphasis: Hungary's Catholic plurality is largely nominal, and a very large share of the population did not state a religious identity at all. Ongoing needs include planting autonomous local churches practicing believer's baptism by immersion and training national pastors.
